description: Ongoing discoveries test the next major bets behind long-haul commercial control, partner continuity, and cargo digital growth.

riskFramework.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
Can customers figure out how to use it?
Can we build it within given resources and constraints?

items.title: Corporate continuity and distribution control discovery
items.name: Corporate continuity and distribution control discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether better corporate content control and deeper partner continuity materially improve programme trust, traveller recovery, and share of wallet in managed travel.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether corporate, agency, and partner-distribution complexity can be reduced enough to make the airline easier to buy and service at account scale.

linkedInitiatives.description: Corporate content control and partner continuity initiative focused on improving corporate, agency & NDC distribution, partner & alliance journey orchestration, offer, pricing & availability management, and operational data, revenue & personalisation platform across NDC and Agency API and Commercial Account Workspace.

assignments.how: Leads managed-travel interviews, channel tradeoff analysis, and the recommendation on where stronger content control matters most.
Provides offer-control and retail-context insight to ensure corporate distribution improvements do not weaken direct-channel economics.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Corporate travel buyers will place more share with the airline if negotiated content and partner continuity become more predictable.
evidenceToCollect: Where account managers and TMCs describe the biggest content and servicing gaps today.
Whether stronger partner continuity would change account confidence enough to shift share.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Better channel control will improve corporate share of wallet and reduce distribution leakage enough to justify deeper investment.
evidenceToCollect: How much revenue is lost through poor negotiated-content visibility or weak continuity.
Which accounts generate enough upside to prioritize first.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Travel managers and agencies can adopt modern distribution more confidently if servicing and exception handling are clearer.
evidenceToCollect: Which NDC or partner-servicing states remain hard to understand.
Where account teams still fall back to manual intervention.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can improve continuity and content control incrementally without forcing a risky all-at-once commercial migration.
evidenceToCollect: Which partner and host dependencies block incremental rollout.
Whether a limited set of account cohorts can pilot the stronger model first.

plannedActivities: Interview corporate account owners, TMCs, and servicing teams about channel and continuity friction.
Quantify distribution leakage and share-of-wallet risk on priority accounts.
Define a pilot cohort for stronger account-aware content control.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to extend the initiative into a broader strategic account programme or keep the work narrowly operational.

items.title: Cargo digital confidence discovery
items.name: Cargo digital confidence discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether broader digital booking coverage and more trustworthy milestone visibility will materially increase digital cargo adoption on premium long-haul lanes.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether freight customers will shift more bookings into digital flows if premium cargo handling and exception visibility become more credible.

linkedInitiatives.description: Cargo digital visibility and premium products initiative focused on improving cargo capacity, quote & booking, cargo tracking & exception management, customer care & case management, and partner & alliance journey orchestration across Cargo Digital Portal and Cargo Connectivity API.

assignments.how: Leads freight-customer learning, digital-booking opportunity sizing, and product recommendation.
Validates which cargo and partner signals can be exposed reliably in the digital experience.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Forwarders will shift more shipments into digital booking if they trust the visibility and exception model for higher-value products.
evidenceToCollect: Which shipment types customers still refuse to place through self-service.
Whether improved milestone and exception clarity changes digital adoption intent.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Lower manual handling cost and stronger premium-product mix will justify deeper digital cargo investment.
evidenceToCollect: What cost can be removed if more bookings and updates move into digital channels.
Which premium products create the largest upside if confidence improves.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Customers can self-serve more bookings if milestone and exception states are explained in operationally meaningful language.
evidenceToCollect: Which shipment and exception terms remain too opaque today.
Where users abandon or revert to manual support.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can raise confidence materially by improving milestone fidelity and case routing without replacing the whole cargo host landscape.
evidenceToCollect: Which milestone gaps are caused by missing source signals versus missing orchestration.
Whether premium-product coverage can expand in a staged rollout.

plannedActivities: Interview forwarders handling pharma, perishables, and high-value shipments.
Map milestone and exception gaps on recent digitally booked shipments.
Define a staged plan to expand premium-product support in the digital cargo journey.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to expand the cargo digital investment into a wider premium-products programme in the next planning cycle.
